title: Closeout transcript segmentation cache
target: Keyword closeout extraction pipeline in src/flg/commands/closeout.py
contract: Preserve extracted decisions, risks, questions, actions, rationale, lessons, goal shifts, relation inference, ordering, patch format, and review behavior.
summary: The closeout path now segments the transcript once for the original-text extractors and once for the code-stripped decision extractor, then reuses those lists across the pipeline.
why_equivalent: The cached lists use the same segmentation and code-stripping operations as before; direct extractor calls retain their original one-argument behavior, and normalized before/after patch sections matched with a fixed hash seed.
complexity_before: Repeated full transcript segmentation across seven extractors plus repeated context-window segmentation.
complexity_after: Two full transcript segmentations per closeout, with shared segment lists for all extractors and decision relation inference.

files.summary: Share original and code-stripped transcript segments across the keyword closeout pipeline.

checks.name: Behavior comparison
checks.detail: All closeout output sections matched between clean baseline and optimized runs with PYTHONHASHSEED=0.

workload: 207 KB campaign-proposal transcript fixture, 2 warmups and 15 measured runs per side, clean baseline source from git archive HEAD, fixed PYTHONHASHSEED=0.
reproduce: python3 scripts/benchmark_command.py --label before-fixed --warmups 2 --runs 15 --cwd /tmp/flg-speedup-baseline-fixed --output /tmp/flg-speedup-before-fixed.json -- env PYTHONHASHSEED=0 PYTHONPATH=/tmp/flg-baseline-src/src .venv/bin/python -m flg.cli closeout --transcript /tmp/flg-speedup-transcript.md --no-llm

python3 scripts/benchmark_command.py --label after-fixed --warmups 2 --runs 15 --cwd /tmp/flg-speedup-optimized-fixed --output /tmp/flg-speedup-after-fixed.json -- env PYTHONHASHSEED=0 PYTHONPATH=/Users/hu/Projects/Oh-My-Project/framing-ledger/src .venv/bin/python -m flg.cli closeout --transcript /tmp/flg-speedup-transcript.md --no-llm
limitations: The workload is a deterministic local transcript and does not include LLM latency or remote I/O.
The fixture is larger than the repository scenarios to expose repeated scans, but production transcript distributions may differ.
residual_risks: The cache is scoped to one closeout call; no cross-session invalidation or persistent cache was introduced.
The original extractor APIs remain compatible, but callers that provide cached segments must provide the matching segmentation variant.
notes: One logical optimization only: reuse transcript segments within closeout.
The result is retained because correctness passed and the measured improvement exceeded the noise threshold.
